IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: TO UNLEASH THE FULL POWER OF PROGRAM-RELATED INVESTMENTS TO ACHIEVE CHARITABLE IMPACT.
IRS990.MissionDesc: TO UNLEASH THE FULL POWER OF PROGRAM-RELATED INVESTMENTS TO ACHIEVE CHARITABLE IMPACT.
IRS990.Desc: VENN FOUNDATION (VENN) IS A NONPROFIT PUBLIC CHARITY ON A MISSION TO UNLEASH THE FULL POWER OF PROGRAM-RELATED INVESTMENTS (PRIS) TO ADVANCE CHARITABLE IMPACT. BY OPENING A DONOR-ADVISED FUND CALLED A "VENN ACCOUNT," ANY INDIVIDUAL OR ORGANIZATION CAN RECOMMEND THAT THEIR CHARITABLE DOLLARS BE USED BY VENN TO MAKE PRIS. VENN CAN SYNDICATE ANY ONE PRI AMONG ANY NUMBER OF VENN ACCOUNTS. FINANCIAL RETURNS FROM THESE PRIS GO BACK TO PARTICIPATING ACCOUNTS PRO RATA FOR THE DONORS TO RECOMMEND REDEPLOYMENT INTO NEW PRIS OR GRANTS. OUR VISION IS TO CREATE A NEW CATEGORY OF FLEXIBLE, BELOW-MARKET-RATE CAPITAL THAT CAN BE DIRECTED CREATIVELY TO PROJECTS AND ORGANIZATIONS ACROSS ALL SECTORS THAT ARE HELPING ADVANCE CHARITABLE GOALS.

InvstProgramRelatedOrgGrp.Desc: KINDLY TECH, INC FOR FUTURE EQUITY
TAYLOR'D SKIN & CO ESTHESTICS BAR, LLC

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: VENN FOUNDATION IS EXEMPT FROM FEDERAL AND STATE INCOME TAXES UNDER SECTION 501(C)(3) OF THE INTERNAL REVENUE CODE AND SIMILAR STATE INCOME TAX LAWS. THE FOUNDATION IS A PUBLIC CHARITY AND CONTRIBUTIONS TO THE FOUNDATION QUALIFIES AS A CHARITABLE TAX DEDUCTION BY THE CONTRIBUTOR. THE FOUNDATION'S TAX RETURNS ARE SUBJECT TO REVIEW AND EXAMINATION BY FEDERAL, STATE AND LOCAL AUTHORITIES.
CHANGE IN VALUE IN CLOSED LOOP FUND -9,235.
MOST GRANTS ARE MADE FROM DONOR ADVISED FUNDS. BOOKS AND RECORDS ARE KEPT SUBSTANTIATING ALL GRANTS. IN ADDITION, RECIPIENTS ARE CHECKED TO MAKE SURE THEY ARE ELIGIBLE PUBLIC CHARITIES.
COLUMN (B) REPORTS THE NUMBER OF CONTRIBUTORS

VENN FOUNDATION MADE A GRANT TO A PUBLIC CHARITY THAT ALSO HAS A DONOR ADVISED FUND WITH THE FOUNDATION. AS A PUBLIC CHARITY, THE DONOR IS NOT A DISQUALIFIED PERSON FOR PURPOSES OF IRC SECTION 4958, AND IS ALSO SPECIFICALLY EXCLUDED UNDER 4966(C)(2).

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION A, LINE 1A
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: IN 2025, THE BOARD EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE WAS COMPRISED OF THE BOARD CHAIR, THE BOARD TREASURER, AND THE PRESIDENT/CEO/SECRETARY.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 11B
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE RETURN IS PREPARED BY THE ORGANIZATION'S INDEPENDENT ACCOUNTANT BASED ON DATA PROVIDED BY MANAGEMENT. ONCE A DRAFT RETURN IS AVAILABLE, IT IS PROVIDED TO THE GOVERNING BODY FOR REVIEW PRIOR TO FILING.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 12C
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY COVERS ANY DIRECTOR, PRINCIPAL OFFICER, OR MEMBER OF A COMMITTEE WITH GOVERNING BOARD DELEGATED POWERS. IF A CONFLICT OF INTEREST WERE TO ARISE, THE INTERESTED PARTY IS NOT PRESENT DURING THE DISCUSSION AND VOTE ON THE TRANSACTION OR ARRANGEMENT INVOLVING THE POSSIBLE CONFLICT OF INTEREST. IF THIS WERE TO OCCUR, IT WILL BE CAPTURED IN THE MEETING MINUTES. EACH YEAR, BOARD MEMBERS ARE ASKED TO COMPLETE A CONFLICT OF INTEREST FORM. THEN IF CONFLICTS OF INTEREST ARISE DURING THE YEAR, THEY ARE TO DISCLOSE TO THE BOARD.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 15
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: USING THE COUNCIL ON FOUNDATION'S 2025 MIDWEST GRANTMAKER SALARY, BENEFITS, & DEMOGRAPHICS REPORT AS A RESOURCE, THE EXECUTIVE OFFICER'S COMPENSATION IS DETERMINED BY THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS. THE DECISION TO APPROVE THE EXECUTIVE OFFICER'S COMPENSATION IS DOCUMENTED IN THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS MEETING MINUTES AS PART OF THE ANNUAL BUDGET REVIEW AND APPROVAL PROCESS. THE PROCESS DESCRIBED HERE WAS LAST COMPLETED IN 2025.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION C, LINE 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE ORGANIZATION DOES NOT MAKE THE GOVERNING DOCUMENTS, CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY, AND FINANCIAL STATEMENTS AVAILABLE TO THE PUBLIC.
CHANGE IN VALUE IN CLOSED LOOP FUND -9,235.
THE PROCESS HAS NOT CHANGED FROM PRIOR YEAR.
